creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
(In reply to Cy Schubert from comment #5)
/dev/null :)  There is no /var/db/ports/net_ntp/options.  We're old school and use portconf which has a separate file for option settings.  I guess that's why I included the options that are set, in the original PR; these are on, everything else is off.

     DOCS=on: Build and/or install documentation
     EXAMPLES=on: Build and/or install examples
     IPV6=on: IPv6 protocol support
     SSL=on: SSL protocol support

from a ports.conf record of 
net/ntp: WITHOUT_THREADS | WITHOUT_PERL_UTILS | WITH_SSL | WITHOUT_NTP_SIGND | WITHOUT_SIGNING | WITHOUT_NLS | WITHOUT_ATOM | WITHOUT_NMEA 

My CFLAGS is
-O2 -pipe -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL2 -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL3 -g0 -ggdb0 -DSTRIP_FBSDID -UDEBUGGING -UDEBUG -fstack-protector-strong -Wl,-z,now -Wl,-z,noexecstack -march=core2 -mtune=core2  -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc5 -fno-strict-aliasing

Since the PR I've also built using the modified Makefile with OPENSSLINC and OPENSSLLIB and compiled cleanly with both clang and gcc5.  
 
Aside: With gcc5, we included the desired options of LDFLAGS+= -Wl,-z,relro -Wl,-z,now -Wl,-z,noexecstack which is where I've been heading with the ntp port and is in production.

If you're able to build ntp via make on FreeBSD 11, then the problem must be somewhere else in the ports infrastructure or possibly /usr/src (?!?!?)
